quotes.text: Martha taught me a lot of things, but the most important is that you gotta fold your bath towels in thirds. If you fold them in halves, you ain't living right. Thirds is pure luxury, man.
quotes.speaker: Snoop Dogg
quotes.context: Red carpet interview at the MTV Video Music Awards, 2022
quotes.fake_note: Snoop and Martha Stewart's friendship is famously documented, making this wholesome domestic advice entirely plausible. But he never gave this specific dissertation on towel-folding geometry.

quotes.text: We spent six months in Cupertino trying to engineer a perfectly silent alarm clock. We ultimately realized that is, by definition, just a broken clock, but the brushed aluminum prototypes were absolutely gorgeous.
quotes.speaker: Tim Cook
quotes.context: Apple Worldwide Developers Conference keynote opener, 2018
quotes.fake_note: It perfectly mimics Apple's obsessive design philosophy and Cook's earnest delivery of minor hardware details. The joke is a classic tech-bro logic failure disguised as innovation.

quotes.text: Technically, when you drop your phone on your face in bed, it isn't falling. The Earth and your face are accelerating upward to meet the phone. It doesn't hurt any less, but the astrophysics are beautiful.
quotes.speaker: Neil deGrasse Tyson
quotes.fake_note: Tyson is notorious for pedantically ruining movies and daily phenomena with physics facts. This mimics his exact cadence, though he never tweeted this specific bedtime observation.

quotes.text: I think this is the most extraordinary collection of talent, of human knowledge, that has ever been gathered together at the White House, with the possible exception of when Thomas Jefferson dined alone.
quotes.speaker: John F. Kennedy

quotes.text: People ask what scares me. Ghosts? Demons? No. It's those automatic paper towel dispensers that make you wave your hands like a desperate wizard just to get a single, damp napkin.
quotes.speaker: Stephen King
quotes.context: Interview on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, 2021
quotes.fake_note: King famously draws horror from mundane everyday objects, making this completely on-brand for his conversational humor. However, he has never actually ranted about bathroom dispensers.
